title: Epic 02 -- Spec Layer and Contract System (fresh)
description: Implement eval spec schemas, contract schemas, YAML/SKILL.md parsing, fixtures, and docs. Reference: 000-docs/epics/epic-02-spec-layer-and-contract-system.md

title: Epic 10 -- Team Product, Eval Packs, and Drift Operations
description: Turn J-Rig Binary Eval into a team-operable product. First real team-facing read surfaces, reusable eval packs for common Claude Skill categories, scheduled drift reevaluation, and obsolete-review workflows. System runs over time by teams, not only individual developers in local/CI mode. Final epic of the 10-epic v1 build. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-10-team-product-eval-packs-and-drift-operations.md
close_reason: Epic 10 complete: drift detection, eval packs, reevaluation scheduling, 151 tests. All 10 epics shipped.

title: Epic 09 -- Optimizer and Experiment Engine
description: Build the controlled improvement engine. Uses prior evaluation evidence to identify highest-value failure areas, propose exactly one interpretable change at a time, execute through the real harness, and decide whether to accept, reject, or revert based on blocker safety, sacred regressions, pack-level effects, and baseline value. Disciplined experiments, not manual guesswork or uncontrolled rewrites. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-09-optimizer-and-experiment-engine.md
close_reason: Epic 09 complete: failure clustering, experiment engine, early stopping, 144 tests

title: Epic 08 -- Regression, Baseline, Scoring, CLI, and CI Gate
description: Turn the harness into real rollout governance. Compare runs over time, enforce sacred regression and blocker rules, compare skill-on vs no-skill baseline, aggregate into transparent launch-readiness outputs, expose usable CLI flows, wire into CI. Make and enforce pass/warn/block/obsolete-review decisions. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-08-regression-baseline-scoring-cli-and-ci-gate.md
close_reason: Epic 08 complete: regression, baseline, scoring, launch reports, 129 tests

title: Epic 07 -- Judgment Layer, Calibration, and Model Matrix
description: Build the external evaluation layer that turns observed outcomes into strict, machine-readable judgments. Establishes deterministic-first grading, binary LLM-judge outputs, calibration against golden cases, disagreement handling, and per-model judgment execution. By the end, J-Rig Binary Eval should say with evidence whether a criterion passed, failed, or is genuinely unclear, in a way later scoring, regression, and optimizer flows can trust. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-07-judgment-layer-calibration-and-model-matrix.md
close_reason: Epic 07 complete: judgment engine, calibration, provider interface, 112 tests

title: Epic 06 -- Functional Execution Harness and Observation Layer
description: Build the functional execution harness. Simulate skill invocation, capture outputs and artifacts, record observed outcomes and execution metadata. First layer testing what skills actually do after selection. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-06-functional-execution-harness-and-observation-layer.md
close_reason: Epic 06 complete: execution runner, observed outcomes, artifact capture, 102 tests

title: Epic 05 -- Trigger Harness and Skill Roster Simulation
description: Build the trigger evaluation system. Available-skills roster simulation, sibling context, trigger test cases, precision/recall/confusion metrics, pack-level overlap analysis. First model-backed behavioral eval layer. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-05-trigger-harness-and-skill-roster-simulation.md
close_reason: Epic 05 complete: roster builder, trigger runner, metrics, confusion detection, 93 tests

title: Epic 04 -- Evidence Layer, Persistence, and Run Lifecycle
description: Create the durable memory of J-Rig Binary Eval. Persistence model, SQLite-first storage, run lifecycle states, filesystem artifact layout, evidence serialization, query/readback helpers. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-04-evidence-layer-persistence-and-run-lifecycle.md
close_reason: Epic 04 complete: SQLite persistence, run lifecycle, evidence CRUD, query helpers, 78 tests

title: Epic 03 -- Package Integrity and Deterministic Checks
description: Build the zero-API-cost validation layer. Catch cheap, obvious, and structural failures before model calls. Package integrity checker, deterministic check registry, description heuristics, referenced file validation. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-03-package-integrity-and-deterministic-checks.md
close_reason: Epic 03 complete: package checker, deterministic registry, heuristics, reporting, 58 tests, all gates pass

title: Epic 02 -- Spec Layer and Contract System
description: Define the formal language of J-Rig Binary Eval. Schema, validation rules, parsing utilities, and authoring expectations for eval specs and eval contracts. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-02-spec-layer-and-contract-system.md
close_reason: Duplicate Epic 02 from partial earlier attempt. Superseded by j-rig-binary-eval-ejj.

title: Epic 02 -- Spec Layer and Contract System
description: Define the formal language of J-Rig Binary Eval. Create schema, validation rules, parsing utilities, and authoring expectations for eval specs and eval contracts. This is the control plane of the product. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-02-spec-layer-and-contract-system.md
close_reason: Duplicate Epic 02 parent. Superseded by j-rig-binary-eval-2s3.

title: Epic 01 -- Repo Foundation and Operating Standard
description: Establish the canonical repository foundation for J-Rig Binary Eval. Includes repo shape, workspace boundaries, package layout, Node/TypeScript/pnpm baseline, initial quality guardrails, repo-level operating guidance, and repo-local Beads setup. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-01-repo-foundation-and-operating-standard.md
close_reason: Epic 01 complete: pnpm monorepo workspace, TypeScript baseline, quality guardrails, CI updated. All gates pass. AAR at 000-docs/009-OD-REPT-epic-01-aar.md

title: Re-run the cost-leak skills under the multi-sample judge, record before/after verdict distributions, and re-sign
description: Run the 7x protocol on coreweave-gpu-cost-leak-hunter under the current single-sample judge (record the before distribution), then under the multi-sample judge (expect >=6/7 identical verdicts), then sign via the reviewer-gated sign-dogfood-bundle.yml and update the labs scorecard. This is the before/after audit artifact.

title: Finish and wire the OTel judge-sample spans with cost attributes and evaluate Claude Code native telemetry
description: Each of the N judge samples emits a judge.verdict span; join cost (tokens, USD) onto judge spans; wire runtime.criterion.evaluated; evaluate a collector/backend and CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY composition. Namespaces must NOT be under labs.* (CISO binding). Build gated on brendan-gregg + kleppmann review recommendation.

title: Wire the cost meter into the eval loop so j-rig eval reports per-skill tokens and USD per judge model times N samples
description: refiner-core/src/cost.ts (createCostMeter) exists but is unwired. Attach it to the eval loop so j-rig eval prints per-run tokens + USD for trigger + execution + judge, multiplied by N samples, per judge model. This is the data for the model recommendation and the OTel cost-join.

title: Benchmark judge quality-per-dollar across free and paid judges and decouple the judge model from the execution model
description: Decouple the judge model in selectProviders (cli eval.ts) and add Groq/NVIDIA NIM as first-class provider presets; benchmark agreement/stability on cost-leak + node-forensics criteria against a human-anchored gold verdict across NVIDIA NIM llama (free), Groq llama-70b (free), DeepSeek v4-flash (paid); test whether N-sample majority on a free judge beats a single paid judge on stability AND cost. Deliverable: quality-per-dollar table + recommendation.

title: Gate blocker BLOCK decisions on judge agreement meeting the stability threshold in decideRollout
description: In governance/scoring.ts, treat a blocker no as BLOCK only when the judge agreement fraction meets the stability threshold chosen from the Phase-1 research; below threshold the criterion is unstable and must not produce a signable BLOCK.

title: Add N-sample majority voting to the LLM judge with agreement-fraction confidence and additive samples/judge_temperature config
description: Wrap the single provider.judge call in judgment/engine.ts in an N-sample loop with a majority reducer; emitted verdict = majority, confidence = real agreement fraction. Add additive Zod config (samples, judge_temperature) on EvalSpecSchema and CriterionSchema and thread through judgeCriteria options.

title: Make the LLM judge noise-robust so signed binary verdicts are stable and reproducible
description: A nondeterministic LLM judge makes j-rig's binary SHIP/BLOCK verdicts unstable: coreweave-gpu-cost-leak-hunter flip-flops SHIP<->BLOCK across identical re-runs because the judge is called once per criterion (un-seeded, temperature 0) and decideRollout blocks on any single blocker no. An unstable verdict cannot honestly be signed and published on labs. Fix: N-sample majority voting per criterion, agreement-fraction confidence, an agreement-threshold stability gate, judge-model decoupling + value benchmark, per-skill eval cost reporting, and OTel sample-level audit spans. Research (academic + practitioner) runs in parallel and sets the threshold + seed-vs-majority decisions.
notes:
GitHub: jeremylongshore/j-rig-skill-binary-eval#183 — https://github.com/jeremylongshore/j-rig-skill-binary-eval/issues/183. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note j-rig-binary-eval-6yq` and `bd-sync close j-rig-binary-eval-6yq` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.
Multi-sample majority judge + stability gate implemented and shipped as PR #184 (branch feat/noise-robust-judge, commit bf03c0e). 1278 tests green locally. Next: 7x before/after stability protocol on coreweave-gpu-cost-leak-hunter, then judge value benchmark + cost meter.

title: Harden the DeepSeek judge-verdict parsing — structured {verdict: yes|no} replies are mis-bucketed as unsure, inflating NO-SHIP
description: With --provider deepseek, the judge model returns the structured JSON it is asked for (ec-1-single-completion.ts:51 instructs 'Reply with a JSON object: {verdict, reasoning}'), but the published CLI parses many of those replies to verdict=unsure with the raw JSON string dumped into reasoning. Reproduced 2026-06-30 on Xquik-dev hermes-tweet (12/24 unsure) and x-twitter-scraper (9/24 unsure): rows whose stored message is literally '{"verdict": "yes", "confidence": 1.0, "reasoning": ...}' are bucketed warning/unsure — i.e. clear passes counted as unsure. The unsure inflation contributes to the spurious block. Note the model also emits a 'confidence' field not in the verdict schema (verdict+reasoning) — the parser should tolerate extra keys. Fix: make the judge-response parser robustly extract verdict from a JSON object (tolerate extra keys, code fences, leading prose) before falling back to unsure; add provider-judge parse tests with deepseek-shaped responses. Surfaced via intent-eval-lab iel-fta (Xquik dogfood). Sibling of j-rig-binary-eval-908.
close_reason: Fixed by PR #175 (MERGED 2026-06-30): extractVerdict() regex fallback recovers verdicts from truncated/fenced JSON + anthropic-real judge budget 256->2048. All CI green, full suite 1239 tests.

title: Ship the per-test-case criteria_ids scoping fix (selectCriteriaForTestCase) in the published jrig-cli — it is in source but absent from 0.1.0/0.1.1
description: The published @intentsolutions/jrig-cli (0.1.0 and 0.1.1) applies EVERY criterion to EVERY test case (cross-product), so an off-topic should_not_trigger control prompt fails unrelated criteria. This is the 'false-blocker bug that inflated NO-SHIP rates' that packages/cli/src/commands/eval.ts:350-371 already fixes via selectCriteriaForTestCase + criteria-selection.ts — but grep of the installed published dist shows 0 occurrences of selectCriteriaForTestCase, i.e. the fix is unshipped. Reproduced 2026-06-30 dogfooding the published CLI on two real external skills (Xquik-dev hermes-tweet, x-twitter-scraper): control prompts 'capital of France' and 'Hamlet plot summary' failed X-safety criteria (action-gated, extraction-estimate, mcp-endpoint), driving a spurious block. Fix: cut a jrig-cli release carrying the scoping fix; add a regression test asserting a should_not_trigger case is not judged against off-scope criteria. Surfaced via intent-eval-lab iel-fta (Xquik dogfood).
close_reason: CORRECTION: the selectCriteriaForTestCase scoping fix is NOT absent from 0.1.1 — it shipped in 0.1.1 via jrig #162. I reproduced the false-blocker on the ccp-pinned 0.1.0 (which predates #162). The real fix is the ccp pin bump 0.1.0->0.1.1 (claude-code-plugins PR #925), not a j-rig change. No j-rig action needed; #162 already added the per-test-case scoping + its test.

description: Apply proposed change in isolated experiment context and run relevant evaluation suite. Must use the real harness and evidence model, not an imitation path. title: 09.5 -- Implement accept, reject, and revert decisions
description: Decision logic for keeping, rejecting, or reverting candidate changes. Must respect all governance rules: blocker failures still block, sacred regressions still matter, pack-level regressions still count, baseline value still matters. Decision output must be explicit enough for durable experiment history. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-09-optimizer-and-experiment-engine.md
close_reason: evaluateExperiment returns accepted/rejected based on before/after comparison

title: 09.3 -- Build the structured one-change proposal engine
description: Propose exactly one atomic change at a time. Explicit, attributable, reversible. Allowed classes: edit description, add example, rewrite instruction, tighten trigger boundary, etc. Optimizer credibility depends on never becoming a sloppy multi-change rewrite machine. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-09-optimizer-and-experiment-engine.md
close_reason: ChangeProposal: one atomic change per experiment

title: 09.1 -- Build failure clustering and failure-pattern grouping
description: Group raw failures into meaningful clusters so the optimizer is not reacting to isolated noise. Identify patterns such as repeated description failures, trigger confusion, unstable functional behavior, or pack-level conflicts. Turn them into structured optimization targets. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-09-optimizer-and-experiment-engine.md
close_reason: clusterFailures groups by method, ranks by severity

title: 09.2 -- Rank weak criteria and select the next optimization target
description: Prioritization logic that decides what to improve next. Use failure rate, blocker severity, sacred regression sensitivity, instability, pack-level confusion, and baseline-value impact to pick the most valuable target rather than chasing the loudest metric. Reference doc: 000-docs/epics/epic-09-optimizer-and-experiment-engine.md

description: Implement the non-negotiable guardrails that stop a rollout when sacred regression cases break or blockers newly fail. title: M4 Phase 2c: score-card scoring + Decision Record draft generator
description: packages/core/src/providers/score-card/: computeProviderScoreCard reads ECSuiteResult + StaticAnalysisInputs (typeSafetyScore 0-3, adapterLoc) + optional CISO gate pass/fail, produces ProviderScoreCard with the 4 PB-7 § 5 rubric dimensions (R5.1 type, R5.2 LOC, R5.3 request-side coverage, R5.4 runtime error categories) + total/36 + cisoGateFailures flag list. draftDecisionRecordFragment emits markdown with CISO gate table + rubric scores + per-EC pass counts + 'council fills in' section (choice, rationale, dissent, memos, tiebreaker, license audit). PURE module — same input always same output (protection against PB-7 § 12 retro-fit anti-pattern). 18 new vitest cases. Closes the eval loop: prototype → run ECs → score → draft DR fragment.
close_reason: Done. 18 new vitest. 237/237 total. Lint + typecheck clean. Score-card closes the M4 eval loop.

title: M4 Phase 2b: EC-1..EC-5 eval-case harness (PB-7 execution surface)
description: Codifies the PB-7 measurement protocol § 4 (eval cases) into runnable TypeScript. packages/core/src/providers/eval-cases/: types.ts (ECResult, ECPerModelOutcome, ECRunner, DEFAULT_MODELS), ec-1-single-completion.ts (3-provider structured-output check), ec-2-streaming.ts (chunk-shape + TTFB metrics), ec-3-tool-calling.ts (should-call + should-not-call phases), ec-4-error-categories.ts (5 error triggers + content-policy-refusal-is-not-error distinction), ec-5-batching.ts (concurrency ratio check vs serial baseline), runFullECSuite (orchestrator). Each runner is pure (one EC = one observation); scoring is separate per PB-7 § 5. 11 new vitest cases covering happy path + intentional-fail detection. Total 219 tests passing. Ready for LiteLLM + Vercel AI SDK prototypes to plug in.
close_reason: Done. 11 new vitest. 219/219 total. Lint + typecheck clean. EC-1..EC-5 harness ready.

title: M4 Phase 2a: Provider interface + CISO PASS/FAIL gates G-1, G-2 + test fixtures
description: Authors the contract both upcoming prototypes (LiteLLM, Vercel AI SDK) implement, plus the harness that runs the CISO PASS/FAIL gates per PB-7 § 6. packages/core/src/providers/: types.ts (Provider interface, ChatMessage, CompletionRequest/Result, StreamChunk discriminated union, ToolDefinition, ToolCallResult), errors.ts (ProviderError + 7 closed categories + retryable defaults), ciso-gates/g1-credential-redaction.ts (intercepts process.stdout/stderr.write, greps captured output for 8+ char substrings of test key), ciso-gates/g2-env-var-spillover.ts (monkey-patches child_process.{spawn,spawnSync,exec,execSync,fork} for the invocation duration, inspects spawned envs + defense-in-depth /proc/<pid>/environ snapshot), test-fixtures/clean-provider.ts (reference baseline that PASSES both gates), test-fixtures/leaky-provider.ts (deliberately fails both gates, used to verify gate runners detect failures). 35 new vitest cases covering all of the above.
close_reason: Done. 208/208 vitest pass (35 new). Lint + typecheck clean. Provider scaffolding + CISO gates ready for LiteLLM/Vercel prototypes to consume.

title: M4 PB-7: provider-adapter measurement protocol doc (CTO Q5 binding gate)
description: 000-docs/018-AT-SPEC-pb7-adapter-measurement-protocol-2026-05-12.md. Per ISEDC v1 Q5 CTO binding: this doc must land in repo BEFORE either LiteLLM or Vercel AI SDK prototype is written. 5 eval cases (single completion, streaming, tool calling, error categories, batching) × 4 rubric dimensions (type safety, LOC, request-side coverage, runtime errors). 2 CISO non-negotiable PASS/FAIL gates (credential redaction, env-var spillover). CMO 3+ launch leaderboard providers. GC license audit. Tiebreaker dimension. Decision Record acceptance criteria for the eventual lock.
close_reason: Done. 14-section normative spec at 000-docs/018-AT-SPEC. INDEX updated. PR forthcoming on feat/m4-pb7-adapter-measurement-protocol.

title: M3 Phase 4: cosign --sign integration in j-rig emit-evidence
description: Adds cosign signing path to j-rig emit-evidence CLI, mirroring audit-harness emit-evidence.sh. New flags: --sign / --key / --keyless / --rekor-url / --predicate-body-only / --cosign-bin. Smoke-tested empirically against the live cosign roundtrip from M2->M3 gate clearance — produces a DSSE envelope with payloadType application/vnd.in-toto+json wrapping our predicate at evals.intentsolutions.io/gate-result/v1. 8 new vitest cases (280/280 total) cover commander registration + plain mode + predicate-body-only + error paths (sign without key, malformed JSON, missing required keys, --output to file).
close_reason: Done. 8/8 new vitest pass. 280/280 total. Lint + typecheck clean. Empirically verified with cosign 2.4.1 keypair: signed envelope round-trips through cosign verify-blob-attestation. Inner Statement is v0.1 (cosign default outer wrap) with our predicateType + predicate verbatim. Stacked PR opening on feat/m3-phase4-cosign-sign-integration.

title: M3 Phase 3b: MM-2 through MM-6 checkers + 15 vendor-generic fixtures
description: Phase 3b mechanically completes the MM-1..MM-6 vocabulary against the intent-eval-lab Intentional Mapping template. 5 new checker files: mm-2-shape-drift.ts, mm-3-cooldown.ts, mm-4-side-effect-verification.ts, mm-5-context-augmentation.ts, mm-6-strict-mode-protocol.ts. 15 vendor-generic fixtures (3 each: PASS/FAIL/NOT_APPLICABLE). Registered in MM_CHECKERS so runAllRegisteredCheckers now returns 6 results. Fixture-driven test pattern via describe.each + it.each — adding new fixtures auto-extends test coverage. Per ISEDC v1 Q2: zero real partner data.

title: M3 Phase 2 (PB-8): AGENTS.md parser at packages/core/src/parsers/agents-md-parser.ts
description: Phase 2 of M3 — AGENTS.md structured parser per build journey M3 deliverable list. ~280 LOC + 17 vitest cases. Extracts: optional YAML frontmatter, first H1 title, H2/H3 sections in order, fenced shell-command blocks classified by semantic heading regex (build/test/setup/style/lint/format), top-level bullets from Tools/Capabilities/Constraints sections. Tolerates missing sections + unknown sections + nested bullets. Protected against ## inside fenced code blocks.

title: M3 epic: j-rig Evidence Bundle emission — Zod validator + I/O adapters + emit-evidence CLI
description: Milestone 3 of the build journey per ~/.claude/plans/se-the-council-bubbly-frog.md. Branch: feat/m3-evidence-bundle-emission. Mirrors audit-harness v0.3.0 emission shape into j-rig. Phase 1 (this PR): Zod schema for in-toto Statement v1 + gate-result predicate, reader/writer adapters, emit-evidence CLI command, vitest unit tests. Phase 2 (subsequent PR): AGENTS.md parser, MM-1..MM-6 implementations + fixture traces, OTel instrumentation, signer wrap, v0.15.0 release. Closes existing EB beads jrig-mje (Zod validator) + jrig-mul (I/O adapters).
acceptance_criteria: packages/core/src/schemas/evidence-bundle.ts exports Zod schemas matching intent-eval-lab gate-result.schema.json; packages/core/src/evidence/{reader,writer}.ts read/write bundles; j-rig emit-evidence CLI command wired; vitest unit tests pass; PR opened on feat branch awaiting Gemini review
close_reason: M3 Phase 1 complete (Zod + I/O + CLI emit-evidence + tests + cross-tool compat). 228/228 vitest pass (191 baseline + 37 new). Lint clean. Typecheck clean across all 4 workspace packages. j-rig emit-evidence supports both pipeline (stdin / --input) and direct (--gate-id/--result/etc) modes; produces in-toto Statement v1 whose predicate validates against the M1 spec schema empirically. Cross-tool compat verified: audit-harness gate --json output flows directly into j-rig emit-evidence. Phase 2 deferrals (subsequent PRs): AGENTS.md parser (PB-8), MM-1..MM-6 implementations + fixture traces (vendor-generic per ISEDC v1 Q2), provider adapters (PB-7), cosign signer wrap, OTel exporter wiring beyond stderr emission, v0.15.0 release tag.

title: Epic 04 enhancement: Evidence Bundle input/output adapters
description:
Two new files in packages/db/src/adapters/:
- evidence-bundle-input.ts — parses Evidence Bundle gate-result rows into j-rig's criterion_results table
- evidence-bundle-output.ts — emits j-rig verdict rows BACK into bundle format

This makes j-rig + audit-harness compose: audit-harness emits → j-rig consumes → j-rig adds verdict rows → audit-harness or downstream tools consume. ~2 day added scope to Epic 04 (Evidence Layer).

title: Migrate j-rig tsconfigs off deprecated baseUrl for TypeScript 6.0 readiness
description: Why this issue exists: TypeScript 6.0 dropped support for the legacy 'baseUrl' tsconfig option without 'ignoreDeprecations: "6.0"'. The j-rig pnpm monorepo's tsconfigs use baseUrl + paths for workspace cross-package imports. Dependabot PR #31 attempted the bump and CI failed with 'TS5101: Option baseUrl is deprecated'. Two paths: (a) add ignoreDeprecations as temporary workaround OR (b) migrate to package exports + import maps. (b) is the right answer for TS 7.0 readiness. PR #31 closed; will reopen after migration.

title: Future Epic 06 interface: execution-bundle context
description:
Future-Epic interface (not implementation): packages/core/src/execution/evidence-bundle-context.ts lets skill runs be informed by prior audit-harness gate results. Just the TypeScript interface, not the wire-up.

Phase C scope. Lets j-rig's L4-L7 judgment runs read 'were the L1+L3 gates passing?' from a bundle, gating execution accordingly.
